summary: McKenzie announces that his Rails SEO tips guide is nearly complete, sharing the table of contents covering on-page and off-page optimization techniques. The guide uses his Daily Bingo Cards site as a case study, covering topics like URL structure, metadata, duplicate content, sitemaps, and linkbait strategies. He notes he's been implementing his own suggestions in his Rails application as he writes about them. The post is a brief progress update requesting feedback and sharing.
key_points: The Rails SEO guide covers both on-page optimization (URLs, metadata, duplicate content, sitemaps) and off-page optimization (linkbait, bookmarks, permalinks)
McKenzie practices what he preaches by implementing the SEO techniques in his own Daily Bingo Cards site as he documents them
The guide is structured as a practical resource for Rails developers, using a real case study rather than abstract advice
money_quotes: Too many projects, too little time.
Don't Repeat Yourself… In Your Content
key_insight: Documenting your own SEO implementation process as a guide for other developers serves double duty as both content marketing and a forcing function to actually implement best practices.
